---
title: "nitrix.graph.community"
generated: true
signature-hash: 6bfb9fb21ec88ced
description: "Community-detection primitives."
listing:
  id: nitrix-graph-community-symbols
  contents: "nitrix-graph-community/*.qmd"
  type: table
  fields: [title, description]
  sort: "title"
  filter-ui: false
  page-size: 500
---

::: {.callout-important title="Generated reference stub — fill me"}
Auto-generated scaffold for `nitrix.graph.community`. Fill the prose *outside* the
generated markers; do **not** ship as-is (SPEC §6.5). `check_reference_staleness.py` flags signature drift.
:::

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:__module__ -->
Community-detection primitives.

Houses the modularity-related operators (the *null model*, the
*modularity matrix*, and *coaffiliation*) plus the relaxed-modularity
quality score.  These all share an algebraic shape with graph
Laplacians, but their *role* is community-specific: [`modularity_matrix`](nitrix-graph-community/modularity_matrix.qmd)
is the operator whose eigendecomposition gives Newman-style community
structure, and [`coaffiliation`](nitrix-graph-community/coaffiliation.qmd) is the symmetric outer product of a
community-assignment matrix.  They are kept separate from the graph
Laplacian operators so that those may remain purely about Laplacians.

The relaxed-modularity score has both *dense* and *sparse* call
paths.  The sparse path uses the factorisation

$$
Q = \sum_{i,j} (A_{ij} - \gamma\, k_i k_j / 2m)\,(CC^{\top})_{ij}
  = \operatorname{tr}(A\,CC^{\top}) - \frac{\gamma}{2m}\,\lVert C^{\top} k\rVert^2
$$

so that an [`ELL`](nitrix-sparse.qmd) or
[`SectionedELL`](nitrix-sparse.qmd) adjacency never materialises the
$n \times n$ modularity matrix.  This is the difference between
"runs on small toy graphs" and "runs on a 100k-node mesh" for the
modularity score.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen dims:__page__ -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
| `n` | query points, each an `ndim`-vector coordinate (variadic) |
| `k` | kernel or basis components |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## Symbols

::: {#nitrix-graph-community-symbols}
:::
